stem: In a certain town, 60% of adults own a car and 45% of adults own a bicycle. If 80% of adults own at least one of these two vehicles, what percent of adults own both a car and a bicycle?
explanation: Using the inclusion-exclusion principle: $P(\text{car or bike}) = P(\text{car}) + P(\text{bike}) - P(\text{both})$. So $80\% = 60\% + 45\% - P(\text{both})$, giving $P(\text{both}) = 105\% - 80\% = 25\%$.

distractorRationale.A: Student subtracted the two individual rates: 60% − 45% = 15%.
distractorRationale.B: Student subtracted the union rate from the larger group: 80% − 60% = 20%.
distractorRationale.D: Student subtracted the smaller group from the union: 80% − 45% = 35%.
